title: Pod CrashLoopBackOff after deploy of payment-service
summary: After deployment of payment-service v2.4.1, pods entered CrashLoopBackOff with exit code 1. Logs showed missing DATABASE_URL env var.
remediation: Restored the DATABASE_URL secret from the previous ConfigMap, redeployed. Long-term: added pre-deploy validation that checks required env vars are mounted before rollout.

title: OOMKilled on api-gateway under traffic spike
summary: api-gateway pods OOMKilled when daily traffic peaked at 3x baseline. Heap was sized at 512MB, holding session cache that grew unbounded.
remediation: Raised memory limit to 1.5GB and added LRU eviction with 10k entry cap to the session cache. Added HPA on memory utilization >70%.

title: 5xx burst from checkout-service due to upstream DB connection pool exhaustion
summary: checkout-service returning 503 for 12 minutes. Connection pool to Postgres was saturated; long-running analytics query held connections.
remediation: Killed the runaway analytics session, raised pool size from 20 to 50, added a 30s statement_timeout. Routed analytics workload to read replica.

title: TLS handshake failures across services after cert rotation
summary: Multiple services unable to reach internal mesh after a scheduled cert rotation. Old CA bundle still cached in service pods.
remediation: Rolled all affected deployments to pick up the new CA ConfigMap. Updated cert-manager to trigger a rolling restart automatically on cert renewal.

title: Latency spike on /search endpoint after index rebuild
summary: p99 latency on /search jumped from 120ms to 4.2s after a nightly index rebuild left the new index uncached.
remediation: Pre-warmed the new index by replaying the top 1000 queries from the prior 24h. Added a warm-up step to the rebuild job.

title: Disk pressure on logging node fills /var
summary: Logging node ran out of disk space; container runtime evicted pods. Root cause was log rotation policy not applied to a new daemonset.
remediation: Cleared old logs, applied logrotate policy via kustomize patch. Added disk-utilization alert at 80%.

title: Auth service rejecting valid tokens after JWT secret rotation
summary: auth-service began returning 401 for tokens issued before the JWT signing-key rotation; old tokens not honored during the overlap window.
remediation: Restored the previous signing key as a secondary verifier with 24h overlap window. Documented dual-key rotation procedure in the runbook.

title: Failed deployment due to image pull backoff
summary: Deployment stuck in ImagePullBackOff — the new image tag was pushed but the registry pull-secret had expired.
remediation: Rotated the registry credential and re-applied the imagePullSecret. Added monitoring on registry credential expiry 7 days out.

title: Kafka consumer lag spike on order-events from continuous rebalancing
summary: Consumer lag on the order-events topic ballooned from <1s to 12 minutes over 30 minutes. A misbehaving consumer in the group held its session open while doing slow processing, triggering session timeouts and group rebalances. Each rebalance paused all consumers for 8-15s.
remediation: Identified the slow consumer via 'kafka-consumer-groups --describe' and restarted it. Increased session.timeout.ms from 10s to 45s and max.poll.interval.ms to 5 minutes. Split slow processing into a separate consumer group so other workers couldn't be blocked. Added consumer_lag alerting per-partition with a 60s warn threshold.

title: Redis cache evictions causing 50%+ database load increase
summary: Redis instance hit maxmemory; allkeys-lru eviction kicked in. Cache hit rate dropped from 94% to 61%, causing a 5x database load spike and elevated p99 latency on read paths. No alert fired because evictions are 'normal' — but the rate was anomalous.
remediation: Restarted Redis after capturing memory snapshot. Found a runaway producer publishing 50KB/min of unbounded session entries. Added size-based monitoring on used_memory vs maxmemory ratio. Set a TTL on the leaky key prefix and increased instance memory from 4GB to 8GB.

title: Pod-to-pod DNS resolution failures after coredns pod restart loop
summary: Multiple services started failing internal DNS lookups (NXDOMAIN/SERVFAIL for *.cluster.local). The coredns deployment had two pods crash-looping after a config map update introduced an invalid 'forward .' clause. Cluster DNS effectively had 1/3 capacity, causing intermittent SERVFAIL responses.
remediation: Reverted the coredns ConfigMap to the prior revision via 'kubectl rollout undo'. Restarted affected service pods that had cached the bad responses. Added a CI check that runs 'coredns-corefile-migration --validate' on the ConfigMap before merge.

title: Istio mTLS handshake failures across mesh after intermediate cert expiry
summary: All cross-namespace traffic in the istio mesh started returning UPSTREAM_RESET_BEFORE_RESPONSE_STARTED. The cert-manager intermediate certificate had expired despite renewals being scheduled — the renewal CronJob had been silently failing for 30 days due to an IAM permission change.
remediation: Manually triggered cert renewal via 'cmctl renew'. Restarted istiod and all sidecars to pick up the new cert. Audited all CronJobs that produce no metrics and added Prometheus alerts on cert-manager renewal failures. Set up an external monitor watching certificate expiry from outside the cluster.

title: Cascading 502 burst after hitting payment-gateway rate limit during sale event
summary: A flash sale drove checkout traffic to 8x baseline. Our rate limit with the payment provider was 100 req/s; we hit 380. Provider returned 429 with Retry-After. Naive retry without backoff doubled outbound rate, triggering more 429s, hung connections on our load balancers, and externally-visible 502 Bad Gateway.
remediation: Killed all pending requests at the LB. Implemented a token bucket rate limiter at our edge proxy capping outbound to 95 req/s. Added jittered exponential backoff for 429 responses. Negotiated a higher rate limit with the provider for advertised sale events.

title: Sidekiq queue depth grew to 50k after worker pod evicted by node pressure
summary: Background worker pod was evicted at 2am due to node memory pressure. Default queue depth grew unbounded; transactional emails (welcome, password reset) started arriving 4+ hours late. No alert fired because the queue depth alert was set on absolute count > 100k.
remediation: Scaled worker deployment from 2 to 6 pods to drain backlog. Lowered queue depth alert threshold to 5k with 5min trigger. Set memory requests/limits on worker pods to prevent eviction. Split critical jobs (transactional email) into a dedicated queue with its own SLO and worker pool.

title: Postgres read replica lag spike to 8 minutes during nightly catalog import
summary: A new bulk product import job inserted 2.5M rows in a single transaction at 3am. Primary kept up; replica fell behind by 8 minutes. Read traffic served by replica returned stale data; users saw outdated prices and inventory until 3:48am.
remediation: Killed the job. Rewrote the import to chunk inserts into 5k-row batches with brief sleeps between batches. Set max_wal_senders and wal_keep_size higher. Added monitoring on replication_lag_bytes with 1MB warn / 10MB page thresholds. Routed all post-import reads to primary for 5 min after import completes.

title: Helm install of search-service v3.1 succeeded but pods came up with wrong config
summary: Helm release reported success but pods started with stale config. A nested .Values key had been renamed from 'db.host' to 'database.host', and the unrendered template silently produced an empty string. Search broke for 12 minutes until rollback.
remediation: Rolled back to v3.0 via 'helm rollback'. Added '{{ required "database.host required" .Values.database.host }}' on all critical config values. Added a CI check that runs 'helm template' + a JSON-schema validator before merge. Added a startup probe that fails if DB connection isn't reachable.

title: image-resize service OOMKilled every ~36 hours from native handle leak
summary: image-resize service consistently hit OOMKilled after running 30-40 hours. Memory grew linearly. Root cause: the image processing library leaked native handles when given malformed JPEG inputs (~0.1% of requests). pmap showed unbounded growth in anonymous mappings.
remediation: Added an isolated subprocess per image to bound the leak to per-request scope. Validated input dimensions and content-type before processing. Added a runtime metric on RSS growth rate with alert at >50MB/hr. Patched the library to release native handles on parse failure (filed upstream PR).

title: JVM full GC pauses spiking p99 latency on inventory-svc to 8s
summary: inventory-svc p99 latency spiked from 80ms to 8s during peak hours. JVM GC logs showed 5-7 second full GCs every 90 seconds. Heap was sized at 4GB on G1GC; allocations far exceeded young-gen capacity due to a new feature loading the entire SKU catalog into request-scoped memory.
remediation: Refactored the offending code path to stream catalog data instead of loading it into memory. Tuned G1GC: increased -Xmx to 6GB, set -XX:MaxGCPauseMillis=200 and -XX:G1HeapRegionSize=16M. Added GC pause time as a Prometheus metric with alert at >500ms p95. Set up async-profiler to capture an allocation flame graph on every deploy.

title: CloudFront origin shield outage caused 100% origin traffic spike
summary: CloudFront origin shield in us-east-1 had a regional issue. Cache hit ratio dropped from 91% to 12% as edge nodes started bypassing the shield. Origin traffic spiked 9x. Origin servers ran out of CPU at 95% utilization, returning 503s for ~22 minutes.
remediation: Manually scaled origin from 8 to 24 instances during the incident. Enabled multi-region shield as fallback. Added an alert on origin RPS > 2x baseline. Implemented stale-while-revalidate at the origin so cache nodes serve slightly stale content during shield issues rather than going to origin.

title: HPA hit maxReplicas 50, request queue backing up causing 504s
summary: api-gateway received 4x normal traffic from a new mobile app release. HPA scaled from 12 to 50 (configured max). Pod CPU still at 95%; new requests queued in nginx ingress for 30s+ before timeout. 504 Gateway Timeout errors spiked to 18% of requests.
remediation: Bumped HPA maxReplicas to 100 and node group max to absorb the spike. Added a cluster-wide capacity alert at >70% of node group max. Implemented a cap-and-shed pattern at the gateway: when in-flight requests > N, return 503 Retry-After instead of queuing. Performed a load test against the new mobile traffic pattern to size headroom for the next release.

title: etcd leader election storm during k8s upgrade — kubectl operations slow
summary: During k8s control-plane upgrade from 1.27 to 1.28, etcd quorum members went down sequentially. Leader election cycled every 5-10 seconds for ~6 minutes. kubectl operations timed out; new pod scheduling stopped; existing workloads stayed up but the cluster was effectively read-only for control-plane changes.
remediation: Waited for upgrade to complete (couldn't safely abort). Post-incident: added pre-upgrade etcd backup as a hard-fail step. Increased etcd disk IOPS provisioning; previous instance was IOPS-throttled even at normal load. Added 'etcd_leader_changes_total > 2 in 5min' alert.

title: Datadog agent sidecar OOMKilled, restarting main container too
summary: Datadog agent sidecar in a sharedProcessNamespace pod was OOMKilled because of metric explosion (cardinality bug in a service emitting per-request labels). When the sidecar restarted, the entire pod restarted because of restartPolicy. Main service had 6 unrelated restarts that hour.
remediation: Set memory limits on the sidecar separately and ample (256MB → 1GB). Configured the metric label cap at the agent level. Restructured pod to not share process namespace so sidecar restart doesn't kill main. Added a per-container OOMKill alert separate from pod-level.

title: Mutating admission webhook timeout blocked all deploys for 18 minutes
summary: A mutating admission webhook (custom policy enforcer) became slow due to its own backend DB issues. Webhook took 25-30s per call; default failurePolicy was Fail. All Pod creates failed with 'admission webhook timeout'. 18 minutes of effectively no deployments cluster-wide.
remediation: Patched the webhook to set failurePolicy: Ignore for the affected category of pods. Added explicit timeoutSeconds: 5. Set up health checks on the webhook backend with a dead-man's switch: webhook auto-disables itself if backend is unhealthy. Documented: never set failurePolicy: Fail without robust availability.

title: Postgres lock contention on orders table caused checkout p99 = 14s
summary: A nightly archival job held a SHARE lock on the orders table for 90 minutes. Checkout writes (UPDATE orders SET status='paid') queued behind it. p99 checkout latency went from 250ms to 14s during business hours; some requests timed out at 30s.
remediation: Killed the archival job. Rescheduled archival to a 3am window and used 'SELECT ... FOR SHARE NOWAIT' to fail fast on conflict. Reviewed all SELECT FOR UPDATE/SHARE patterns for unbounded loops. Added pg_stat_activity monitoring with alert on any waiting query > 60s. Set lock_timeout = 5s for OLTP roles.

title: Race condition on OAuth refresh tokens caused 401 burst on mobile clients
summary: Mobile auth backend allowed concurrent refresh-token grant requests; both succeeded but only one was kept active. Clients with the now-invalid token started getting 401. Affected ~3% of users for 4 hours until natural rotation. Spike reached 8000 req/min of 401s.
remediation: Added a per-user advisory lock during refresh-token rotation. Returned 200 for the second concurrent refresh with the cached new token (idempotent within a 5s window). Added 401-rate-per-endpoint monitoring. Mobile clients updated to 'retry-once-on-401-then-relogin' pattern.

title: NetworkPolicy update blocked recommendations-svc → catalog-svc traffic
summary: A team applied a default-deny NetworkPolicy to the recommendations namespace as part of a security audit. They allowed external traffic but missed the internal call to catalog-svc in another namespace. Recommendations API started returning empty results; users saw blank 'you might also like' sections for 2 hours.
remediation: Added an explicit egress rule allowing recommendations → catalog. Implemented a CI check that diffs new NetworkPolicies against current pod-to-service connections (using cilium hubble flows) and warns about flows that would be dropped. Added a synthetic test in the recommendations CI pipeline that validates an end-to-end recommendation request.

title: All deploys failing with ImagePullBackOff after ECR credential rotation
summary: AWS rotated ECR credentials as part of standard policy. The kubelet imagePullSecret was a static dockerconfigjson and didn't auto-refresh. New pods stuck in ImagePullBackOff cluster-wide. Existing pods kept running so no immediate user impact, but no rollouts/scales were possible for 2 hours.
remediation: Manually re-created the imagePullSecret with fresh credentials. Long-term: deployed amazon-ecr-credential-helper as a DaemonSet that refreshes secrets every 6 hours via IAM Roles for Service Accounts (no static creds). Added monitoring on dockerconfigjson age — alert at >12 hours.

title: Node disk pressure evicted prometheus-server during business hours
summary: Prometheus pod was evicted from a node due to DiskPressure (root filesystem at 92% from container logs). Prometheus restart took 18 minutes to replay WAL; alerting was blind during that window. A real database alert that fired during the gap was missed.
remediation: Configured docker log rotation (max-size 100m, max-file 3). Cleaned up old container images on all nodes. Added a separate volume mount for prometheus data on a dedicated EBS volume (not root). Set up an alert on node disk usage > 75%. Configured Prometheus HA pair so a single eviction doesn't cause blindness.

title: Lambda cold starts spiked p99 to 8s during morning traffic ramp
summary: A Lambda function with 1.5GB of dependencies was cold-starting in 6-8 seconds. During morning traffic ramp from 0 → 200 req/s, ~40% of requests hit cold starts; p99 latency spiked to 8s. Provisioned concurrency was set to 5 but the autoscaler hadn't scaled it up.
remediation: Reduced bundle size from 1.5GB to 280MB by removing unused dependencies. Configured Provisioned Concurrency Auto Scaling with target tracking on ProvisionedConcurrencyUtilization=0.7. Set min capacity to 50. Moved init-time work (loading caches, warming connections) to module scope.

title: RDS storage autoscale couldn't keep up; writes failing for 11 minutes
summary: An ETL job started inserting 8GB/hour into a write-heavy table; existing free storage was 4GB. RDS storage autoscale triggered but takes 5-15 minutes to provision the next allocation. When storage hit 100% before autoscale completed, all writes returned 'ERROR: could not extend file'. 11 minutes of write failures.
remediation: Pre-allocated 100GB headroom for the ETL window. Set MaxAllocatedStorage higher and added a storage utilization alert at 80% (well before autoscale threshold). For large bulk operations, use COPY into a dedicated staging table on a separate volume, then merge in chunks. Added a pre-flight storage check to the ETL job that bails out if free space < projected write × 1.5.
